steps.title: Identify the clause relationship
steps.content: The “when” clause is a dependent time clause describing when the main action occurred. The main clause “she became the state's first female chief executive” is in past tense and specifies a date in the past.

steps.title: Apply parallel tense
steps.content: In time clauses paired with a past-tense main clause, the dependent verb must also be past tense. “Began” (past tense) correctly shows the action at that past moment, matching “became.” “Begins,” “will begin,” and “has begun” use present, future, or present perfect tenses that break the parallel structure. That matches choice C — C is correct.
